orcad.help /share/b/pub/softmems.help /share/b/pub/virtuoso.help Jan 28 2021 CONTENTS Cadence on EECS Instructional Computers Scripts for starting Cadence tools Cadence Disclaimer References Errors: 'clsbd' Look Here for Additional

erences Errors: 'clsbd' Look Here for Additional Information Cadence on EECS Instructional Computers --------------------------------------- Cadence (http://www.cadence.com) is a suite of design kits for integrated circuits. Cadence is a

n kits for integrated circuits. Cadence is a registered trademark of Cadence Design Systems, Inc., 2655 Seely Avenue, San Jose, CA 95134. EECS is proud to be a Cadence University Program Member. (http://www.cadence.com/support/university

mber. (http://www.cadence.com/support/university/ww_usp.aspx) Cadence tools are commonly used in EE141, EE142, EE240 and EE241. IC 6.15 is installed for Instructional users on these Linux computers: eda-1.eecs.berkeley.edu (RHEL 64-bit Lin

uters: eda-1.eecs.berkeley.edu (RHEL 64-bit Linux server) eda-2.eecs.berkeley.edu (RHEL 64-bit Linux server) eda-3.eecs.berkeley.edu (RHEL 64-bit Linux server) eda-4.eecs.berkeley.edu (RHEL 64-bit Linux server) eda-5.eecs.berkeley.edu (RHE

4-bit Linux server) eda-5.eecs.berkeley.edu (RHEL 64-bit Linux server) eda-6.eecs.berkeley.edu (RHEL 64-bit Linux server) eda-7.eecs.berkeley.edu (RHEL 64-bit Linux server) eda-8.eecs.berkeley.edu (RHEL 64-bit Linux server) To run Cadence

edu (RHEL 64-bit Linux server) To run Cadence tools from your own computer, login to one of those computers with 'X2Go', which will give you a full graphics console on that computer. An alternative is to login using ssh with X11 forward

ernative is to login using ssh with X11 forwarding enabled, and run an XWindows server such as Xming or Quartz on your computer. Open a terminal window on our computer and enter one of these commands: /share/b/bin/virtuoso6 # for IC 6.1

ommands: /share/b/bin/virtuoso6 # for IC 6.15, the default source /share/b/bin/cds6; virtuoso # for IC 6.17 The 'cds6' script sets the path so 'virtuoso' is found. Your instructor may provide a different script if your class needs a d

provide a different script if your class needs a different version. The GSI for the class should consult with inst@eecs.berkeley.edu about that. See below for more about our wrapper scripts. See http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/connecting.html#l

ee http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/connecting.html#labs for current lists of all of our computers. See http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/connecting.html for information about getting an account, and using XWindows. For help and documentation, c

using XWindows. For help and documentation, click on the "Help" button on each Cadence window, or login to https://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~inst/pub/. Scripts for starting Cadence tools ---------------------------------- You can set your p

---------------------------- You can set your path and libraries for particular versions like this: V_CDS="615.151" V_MMSIM=141 source /share/b/bin/cds6 virtuoso To load the Spectre toolbox for reading results with Matlab: V_CDS="615.151"

or reading results with Matlab: V_CDS="615.151" V_MMSIM=141 source /share/b/bin/cds6 /share/instsww/pkg//matlab-r2016a/bin/matlab The wrapper scripts indicate where Cadence is installed for each operating system and version. It's mostly und

operating system and version. It's mostly under /share/instsww/cadence. Cadence Disclaimer ------------------ Because EECS has created some additional programs to help you start Cadence tools on our systems, Cadence would like us to post

ols on our systems, Cadence would like us to post this disclaimer about their responsibility: "Information is provided 'as is' without warranty of any kind. No statement is made and no attempt has been made to examine the information, eit

pt has been made to examine the information, either with respect to operability, origin, authorship, or otherwise. Please use this information at your own risk. We recommend using it on a copy of your data to be sure you understand what it do

f your data to be sure you understand what it does under your conditions. Keep your master intact until you are satisfied with the use of this information within your environment." References ---------- - manuals on the Instructional WEB s

---------- - manuals on the Instructional WEB site (password required): https://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~inst/pub/nis/?file=Cadence - Documentation System on the Cadence WEB site: http://www.cadence.com/support/education/cdsdoc.aspx -

www.cadence.com/support/education/cdsdoc.aspx - Which tools are available for which operating system: http://www.cadence.com/support/computing/Pages/default.aspx - "Cadence Platform Support Roadmap": http://www.cadence.com/support/compu

Roadmap": http://www.cadence.com/support/computing/Documents/Cadence_Platform_Support_Plan.pdf - documentation in $CADENCE_DIR/doc - the email group cadence-users @ lists.eecs.berkeley.edu - EE141 lab instructions: http://bwrc.e

du - EE141 lab instructions: http://bwrc.eecs.berkeley.edu/classes/icdesign/ee141_f06/CadenceLabs/Lab2/soft_lab2.pdf http://bwrc.eecs.berkeley.edu/classes/icdesign/ee141_f06/SoftwareLabs/Lab2/VirtuosoTutorial.htm - Excellent SKILL scri

ab2/VirtuosoTutorial.htm - Excellent SKILL scripting resource offered by its author (Feb 2008): http://www.geocities.com/theperlconnection//TheCadConnection.pdf http://www.geocities.com/theperlconnection/CadScriptingLanguage_csl.pdf -

eperlconnection/CadScriptingLanguage_csl.pdf - Help pages on auto place and route using Silicon Ensemble by Seth Hollar and on mixed-mode simulations by Michael Scott (Nov 2001). See http://www-bsac.EECS.Berkeley.EDU/~cadence/ Errors: 'c

//www-bsac.EECS.Berkeley.EDU/~cadence/ Errors: 'clsbd' -------------- If you get an error when you try to start virtuoso, such as: "Unable to connect to clsbd on host ..." That is probably because of previous Cadence lock files. When you

cause of previous Cadence lock files. When you aren't running virtuoso, try deleting any residual lock files, which you can list with ls -la ~/CDS*.cdslck Look Here for Additional Information ------------------------------------ This do

on ------------------------------------ This document is under revision to document the IC6 versions on Centos using X2Go (April 2018). Older information on IC5 and other Cadence tools is available at /share/b/pub/cadence-ic5.help.
ilable at /share/b/pub/cadence-ic5.help. EECS Instructional Support 378/384/386 Cory, 333 Soda inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
